Royalty Pharma, Johnson & Johnson in $500 Million Autoimmune Drug Funding Pact
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher


Royalty Pharma has inked a $500 million research-and-development co-funding agreement with Johnson & Johnson to support development of a proposed drug for autoimmune diseases.

Royalty Pharma, a New York biopharmaceutical royalties investor, on Monday said it will provide the funding in 2026 and 2027 to advance the development of Johnson & Johnson's JNJ-4804 drug candidate.

JNJ-4804 is currently in Phase 2 studies for the autoimmune diseases ulcerative colitis, psoriatic arthritis and Crohn's disease.

New Brunswick, N.J., pharmaceutical giant J&J last month struck a deal under which asset manager Blackstone will help fund the development of its bleximenib drug candidate for the blood cancer acute myeloid leukemia.


Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-30-26 0838ET

China Resources Mixc Lifestyle Services Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
China Resources Mixc Lifestyle Services Limited reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was CNY 18,021.62 million compared to CNY 17,154.41 million a year ago. Net income was CNY 3,969.02 million compared to CNY 3,597.96 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.739 compared to CNY 1.576 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.739 compared to CNY 1.576 a year ago.

data.title: EVE Energy's unit plans to invest 6 billion yuan in project
China Eastern Airlines Unveils Plan To Boost Service Capacity For 2026 Summer?Autumn Flight Season
data.description:
China Eastern Airlines recently unveiled its plan to boost service capacity for the 2026 summer?autumn flight season starting March 29, with 823 passenger aircraft, including 14 domestically produced C919 jetliners, operating over 950 routes. Its daily flights are expected to exceed 3,200, up 1.3% year on year, according to the airline. Following its hub-focused strategy, China Eastern Airlines plans to further increase flight frequencies and optimize route network and flight schedules in a bid to strengthen connectivity and transfer efficiency at key hubs in major Chinese cities, including Shanghai, Beijing, Xi?an, Kunming, and Chengdu.

Its premium service network, ?Air Express,? is expected to expand to 47 domestic routes and four international routes. The airline aims to elevate its average weekly international and regional departures to 1,400, with Europe-bound departures exceeding 160 (up 24% year on year), and Southeast Asia-bound departures surpassing 500 (up 13% year on year).

Routes to South Korea, Australia, New Zealand, and other countries and regions will also experience varying increases in weekly departures. A total of 30 new international and regional routes are expected to be launched during the new flight season, including a route between Xi?an, capital of northwest China?s Shaanxi Province, and Vienna, as well as routes connecting Shanghai and Tashkent (starting March 30), Adelaide, Cheongju, and Tbilisi. Flight frequencies will be increased on 14 existing international and regional routes linking Shanghai and global destinations, including Venice, Barcelona, and Singapore.

Around the summer vacation period (July-August), a batch of routes will be resumed or newly introduced, including international and regional routes between Shanghai and Stockholm, Dublin, Zurich, Mumbai, and Ulaanbaatar. The airline also plans to further enhance passenger experience through new and upgraded services. Complimentary in-flight Wi-Fi will be available across all classes on wide-body aircraft operated by China Eastern Airlines and its subsidiary Shanghai Airlines on routes between China and Australia, New Zealand, and surrounding regions.

J&T Global Express Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
J&T Global Express Limited reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was USD 12,157.81 million compared to USD 10,259.1 million a year ago. Net income was USD 198.41 million compared to USD 100.56 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 0.023 compared to USD 0.012 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 0.022 compared to USD 0.006 a year ago.

Blackstone Closes $6.3 Billion Life Sciences Fund
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller


Blackstone closed a $6.3 billion life sciences fund, which it said is the largest life sciences private fund ever raised.

The company said the fund was oversubscribed and closed at its cap.

Blackstone launched its life sciences platform, which has $15 billion in assets under management as of the fourth quarter of last year, in 2018 to invest across companies and products in life science sectors.


Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

Jefferies names DSV a top pick, shares climb
data.description:
Logistics firm DSV rose 1.8 percent after Jefferies designated the company as a top pick and reiterated its buy rating, according to Investing.com.

The bank points to improving freight rates in both air and sea transport and expects yields to follow suit, which is seen supporting the full-year guidance issued prior to recent Iran-related events.

Jefferies also maintains a buy rating for DHL and expects DSV to provide details on the opportunities and risks associated with AI during its capital markets day in May.

Ex-OpenAI's Kass: AI Is Going to Make a Lot of Winners
data.description:
Former OpenAI Head of Go-To-Market Zack Kass believes that while it's still early days for artificial intelligence investments, AI is going to make a lot of winners. Kass has become one of the most sought-after voices on how businesses can harness this powerful technology. He speaks with Haslinda Amin from the sidelines of 'Citi Macro Conference in Hong Kong'.
